learning.summary: TPM.MSC is a Microsoft Management Console (MMC) snap-in used to view Trusted Platform Module information. It helps verify TPM presence, status, and readiness for Windows security features and Windows 11 upgrades.
learning.explanation:
The TPM Management Console is launched with the command tpm.msc. It opens an MMC snap-in that displays information about the Trusted Platform Module installed in a computer.

Administrators can use the console to verify whether a TPM is present, determine its status and version, and confirm that it is ready for use. This information is useful when troubleshooting TPM-related issues or confirming Windows 11 upgrade readiness.

facts.text: tpm.msc opens the TPM Management Console.
The TPM Management Console is an MMC snap-in.
Use TPM.MSC to verify TPM status, version, and readiness.

commands.description: Open the TPM Management Console snap-in.

examples.text: A technician runs tpm.msc to verify TPM 2.0 is enabled before upgrading to Windows 11.

notes: Use this tool with the TPM concept rather than as a replacement for TPM knowledge.

examTips.text: Know that tpm.msc opens the TPM Management Console.

commonMistakes.text: Confusing TPM.MSC with firmware configuration screens.

scenarios.situation: Verify TPM readiness before an operating system upgrade.
scenarios.expectedAction: Identify the correct concept or response.

pbqIdeas.task: Choose the correct Windows tool to inspect TPM status.

reviewNotes: Detailed field descriptions vary by Windows version and should be validated during review.
Promoted from authored draft as needs-review canonical content.
